claim: AI agents are autonomous entities that perceive their environment, make decisions, and execute actions to achieve specific goals, distinguishing them from LLMs which focus on language processing without environmental interaction
actionable: Understand AI agents as systems with perception-decision-action loops tied to specific environments, not just conversational models
source_title: Build with Founders Masterclass - AI Agents

claim: Intent-based agents enable specific domain functionality like DeFi transaction execution through natural language; example: 'send $500 to shantanu' can execute transactions with proper context
actionable: Design agents with hardcoded intents for specific actions (send, receive, swap, bridge) rather than purely conversational responses
source_title: Build with Founders Masterclass - AI Agents

claim: LLM-powered agents reduce training time by leveraging pre-trained models as a base layer, eliminating the need to train from scratch on general knowledge
actionable: Use existing open-source LLMs (Llama, others) as foundation and build agent-specific logic on top rather than training custom models from scratch
source_title: Build with Founders Masterclass - AI Agents

claim: Personalized portfolio tracker agents for DeFi use cases can analyze on-chain data and recommend strategies based on user holdings and market conditions
actionable: Build agents with on-chain data access and fine-tuning on DeFi-specific concepts to enable intelligent portfolio recommendations
source_title: Build with Founders Masterclass - AI Agents

claim: LLMs provide three core capabilities for agents: improving language understanding of context, enhancing communication through nuanced interpretation, and generating responses aligned with user intent
actionable: Prioritize LLM choices based on their ability to understand domain context, interpret user intent accurately, and generate contextually appropriate responses
source_title: Build with Founders Masterclass - AI Agents
